description: A conceptual diagram illustrating the projection of real ground deformation onto Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) Line-Of-Sight (LOS) for both ascending and descending satellite geometries. The diagram shows a Measurement Point (MP) on a ground profile, with a vertical axis labelled 'h' for height and a horizontal axis labelled 'ground range'. A true ground deformation vector, `d_real` (green arrow), originates from the MP. Two satellites, positioned for ascending and descending orbits, observe the MP. The Line Of Sight (LOS) for the ascending pass is labelled 'LOS Asc', and the observed deformation component along this line is `d_Asc` (red arrow). Similarly, the LOS for the descending pass is labelled 'LOS Desc', and the observed deformation component along this line is `d_Desc` (red arrow). The `d_Asc` and `d_Desc` vectors are shown as projections of the `d_real` vector onto the respective LOS directions, with dotted lines indicating the components perpendicular to the LOS. This visualises how the real 3D ground movement is decomposed into 1D Line-Of-Sight measurements from different satellite viewing angles, a key principle for integrating Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) and Interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar (InSAR) data in services like the European Ground Motion Service (EGMS).
